title: Sterculia laurina
intro: Sterculia laurina, also known as the African candelabra tree, is a deciduous tree native to tropical Africa, characterized by its distinctive candelabrum-like branches and large, showy flowers. The health benefits of Sterculia laurina include its use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Sterculia laurina include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, wounds, and gastrointestinal disorders.
taxonomy: Sterculia laurina is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It belongs to the genus Sterculia and is also known as the cotton tree. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics, including its leaves, flowers, and fruit. The plant is native to tropical regions of Africa and Asia. Within the Malvaceae family, Sterculia laurina is one of the many species that are commonly known for their large, showy flowers and edible fruit.

The roots and leaves of the plant are reportedly used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
